router.name: Apple Development Foundation master
router.description: Reference-first catalog for routing Apple-platform tasks to relevant child skills, with optional foundation audits and installer verification.

invocation.note: Explicit invocation is the reliable disambiguator; select child skills according to their own descriptions and exclusions.

procedure: Read this catalog first and shortlist parent skills by ID, role, and purpose.
Read only the shortlisted child SKILL.md files and follow their instructions.
Do not imply an audit, repository scan, installer action, or verification run for ordinary task routing.
Use codex-bootstrap when the request is a new consumer Apple-platform project.
routing.consumer_verification: App-code verification is owned by the selected child skill, such as swift-testing-verification or maestro-apple-app-testing.

discovery: Read the current project's AGENTS.md and local skill directories.
Read .agents/skills/manifest.json when auditing this foundation repository.
Resolve each parent skill path relative to the foundation repository root.
comparison: Compare skill IDs first, then resolve paths and inspect frontmatter and semantic contracts.
Classify differences using the definitions below; do not use conflicting as an audit classification.

classifications.missing: A catalog parent skill absent from the target inventory.
classifications.extra: A target-local skill outside the catalog that is not intentionally external.
classifications.stale: A matching skill ID whose resolved path, frontmatter, or catalog contract is outdated or broken.
classifications.duplicate: Multiple target entries representing the same catalog skill.
classifications.external: An intentionally documented third-party skill outside this repository's catalog; exclude it from missing and extra findings.

audit.verification_scope: Run these commands only when auditing this foundation repository or its installer; they do not verify consumer app code.

router.example_prompt: Use $apple-development-foundation to route this iOS import-flow task to the relevant child skills. Read the catalog first, then read only the selected child SKILL.md files; do not audit or run foundation verification.

skills.name: apple-development-foundation
skills.description: Use to reference-first route an Apple-platform task to this repository's relevant child skills, or when an audit or comparison is explicitly requested. Do not treat it as a replacement for child instructions, an implied repository audit, or proof of automatic activation.
skills.purpose: Reference-first skill router with optional foundation audits
skills.use_for: reference-first child-skill routing and explicitly requested foundation audits
skills.do_not_use_for: replacing child instructions, implied audits or verification, or claiming automatic activation

skills.name: codex-bootstrap
skills.description: Use when bootstrapping a new iOS or macOS SwiftUI project or major feature set in the consumer workspace. Chain skills from this repo (design system, components, reviews). Do not use for one-off screens, editing this repo's archived package, or rebuilding AppleDevelopmentFoundation archive/Sources unless explicitly asked.
skills.purpose: Bootstrap a new consumer iOS or macOS SwiftUI project and chain relevant foundation skills
skills.use_for: bootstrapping new iOS or macOS projects in the consumer workspace using these skills
skills.do_not_use_for: one-off screens or editing archived package code in this repository

skills.name: apple-platform-planner
skills.description: Use when planning a new iOS, macOS, or shared Apple-platform feature. Do not use for implementation-only requests or non-Apple planning.
skills.purpose: Plan a new iOS, macOS, or shared Apple-platform feature

skills.name: swiftui-tab-navigation
skills.description: Use when adding, replacing, or refactoring an app-level SwiftUI tab shell, including adopting the system Liquid Glass tab-bar appearance on iOS 26. Do not use for page-style TabView content, segmented controls, business-specific screen implementation, or navigation that has no peer destinations.
skills.purpose: Add or refactor native app-level SwiftUI tab navigation, including system Liquid Glass behavior
skills.use_for: native app-level SwiftUI tabs and system Liquid Glass tab chrome
skills.do_not_use_for: page-style tabs, segmented controls, or business-specific screens

skills.name: swiftui-component-author
skills.description: Use when creating or refactoring a reusable SwiftUI component. Do not use for business-specific screens, non-UI services, or design-token-only changes.
skills.purpose: Create or refactor reusable SwiftUI components

skills.name: apple-design-system
skills.description: Use when adding or changing reusable design-system tokens or components. Do not use for application branding, app-specific screens, or a general accessibility audit.
skills.purpose: Add or change reusable semantic design-system tokens and components

skills.name: apple-app-marketing-site
skills.description: Use when creating, re-theming, polishing, or migrating a static Astro + Tailwind marketing site for an iOS or macOS app (landing, help, support, privacy, terms). Do not use for SwiftUI app UI, App Store screenshot sets, or general web apps unrelated to Apple product marketing.
skills.purpose: Build or re-theme a static Astro marketing site for an Apple consumer app
skills.use_for: Apple app marketing sites (Astro + Tailwind): scaffold, polish, retheme, deploy
skills.do_not_use_for: SwiftUI app UI, App Store screenshots, or general non-marketing web apps

skills.name: ios-macos-platform-adaptation
skills.description: Use when an Apple-platform feature genuinely behaves differently on iOS and macOS. Do not use when native behavior is identical or when only planning a new feature.
skills.purpose: Adapt concrete behavior that genuinely diverges between iOS and macOS

skills.name: swift-package-module-author
skills.description: Use when adding an independently importable Swift Package module in the active workspace. Do not use for an app-local feature, extraction review, or editing AppleDevelopmentFoundation archived package code unless explicitly asked.
skills.purpose: Add an independently importable Swift Package module in the active workspace
skills.use_for: a reusable package target in the active workspace
skills.do_not_use_for: an app-local feature or archived package work in this repository

skills.name: reusable-code-extractor
skills.description: Use when extracting a proven implementation from an application into a shared module or package in the consumer workspace. Do not use for speculative abstraction, landing code in the AppleDevelopmentFoundation archived package, or an app-local refactor that should stay local.
skills.purpose: Extract proven application code into a neutral shared module or package
skills.use_for: generalizing proven app code into consumer shared modules
skills.do_not_use_for: speculative extraction or landing code in this repository's archived package

skills.name: swift-concurrency-review
skills.description: Use when reviewing async/await, actors, tasks, observation, Sendable diagnostics, or cancellation. Do not use for unrelated UI styling or synchronous API design.
skills.purpose: Review async/await, actors, observation, Sendable correctness, and cancellation

skills.name: apple-accessibility-review
skills.description: Use when reviewing reusable SwiftUI UI for accessibility. Do not use for non-UI code, general design-token work, or pixel-only critique.
skills.purpose: Review reusable SwiftUI UI for accessibility and platform interaction

skills.name: apple-security-privacy-review
skills.description: Use when reviewing storage, file access, logging, permissions, imports, or sensitive data. Do not use for purely visual changes or unrelated API style review.
skills.purpose: Review storage, file access, logging, permissions, imports, and sensitive data
skills.use_for: files, logging, permissions, and sensitive data

skills.name: swift-testing-verification
skills.description: Use after a meaningful Apple-platform code change to select and run verification. Do not use for planning-only work or before an implementation exists.
skills.purpose: Select and run focused verification after meaningful Apple-platform changes

skills.name: maestro-apple-app-testing
skills.description: Use when building, debugging, authoring, or thoroughly testing an Apple app with Maestro, including iOS simulator flows, UI inspection, screenshots, flaky-flow diagnosis, and end-to-end regression coverage. Do not use for non-testing product implementation or standalone unit tests.
skills.purpose: Build, inspect, and test Apple apps with Maestro end-to-end workflows
skills.use_for: thorough Maestro testing for Apple apps

skills.name: codex-skill-maintainer
skills.description: Use when creating, reviewing, splitting, updating, or validating this repository's local Codex skills. Do not use for ordinary product code changes or global-skill installation.
skills.purpose: Create, review, update, and validate this repository's local skills
